title: Systems and methods for automatic range control
abstract: An output control for a welding-type power supply includes a first contactor associated with a first weld output range. A second contactor associated with a second weld output range. An input device configured to select a weld output value from the first weld output range and the second weld output range. A control circuit configured to control the first contactor and the second contactor based on a selected weld output value, and adjust a weld output based on the selected weld output value of the input device.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An output control for a welding-type power supply comprising: a first contactor associated with a first weld output range; a second contactor associated with a second weld output range; an input device configured to select a weld output value from the first weld output range and the second weld output range; and a control circuit configured to: control the first contactor and the second contactor based on a selected weld output value; and adjust a weld output based on the selected weld output value of the input device. 2. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the control circuit is configured to engage the second contactor and disengage the first contactor in response to the selected weld output value corresponding to the second weld output range. 3. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the control circuit is configured to transition from the first weld output range to the second weld output range when the selected weld output value is within the second weld output range. 4. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the control circuit is configured to transition from the second weld output range to the first weld output range when the selected weld output value is within the first weld output range. 5. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the input device is a single knob configured to select the weld output value. 6. The control as defined in claim 1, further comprising a display to provide an indication of a value of the weld output based on an engaged contactor and a selected weld output value. 7. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the first weld output range overlaps the second weld output range. 8. The control as defined in claim 1, further comprising a third contactor associated with a third weld output range, the control circuit further configured to: control the third contactor based on the selected weld output value; and adjust a weld output within the third weld output range based on the selected weld output value. 9. The control as defined in claim 8, wherein the second weld output range overlaps the third weld output range. 10. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the control circuit is configured to control the welding system to output a substantially linear and continuous increase through the first weld output and the second weld output range. 11. The control as defined in claim 1, wherein the control circuit is configured to adjust the weld output within each of the first weld output range and the second weld output range by changing a field current of an engine-driven generator. 12. The control as defined in claim 11, wherein the control circuit is configured to: increase the weld output by controlling the contactors and increase the field current; and decreases the weld output by controlling the contactors and decrease the field current. 13. A method of controlling a welding-type power supply, comprising: controlling a first contactor associated with a first weld output range and a second contactor associated with a second weld output range based on a first input from an input device; selecting a weld output value via the input device; and adjusting a weld output within the first weld output range or the second weld output range based on the selected weld output value. 14. The method as defined in claim 13, wherein the input device is a selector knob operatively connected to a control circuit configured to control the first and second range contactors. 15. The method as defined in claim 13, further comprising: engaging the second range contactor corresponding to the second weld output range in response to the selected weld output value corresponding to the second weld output range; and disengaging the first contactor in response to the selected weld output value. 16. The method as defined in claim 13, further comprising transitioning from the first weld output range to the second weld output range when the selected weld output value is within the second weld output range. 17. The method as defined in claim 16, further comprising transitioning from the second weld output range to the first weld output range when the selected weld output value is within the first weld output range. 18. The method as defined in claim 13, wherein the first weld output range overlaps the second weld output range. 19. The method as defined in claim 18, further comprising: controlling a third contactor associated with a third weld output range based on the selected weld output value; and adjusting a weld output within the third weld output range based on the selected weld output value. 20. The method as defined in claim 13, further comprising: comparing a measured weld output value to the selected weld output value; determining whether a change from measured weld output value to the selected weld output value will result in a higher output or a lower output; disengaging the first engage the second in response to higher output; and disengaging the second and engage the first in response to the low output.
